creator: webbusy.tech@gmail.com
text:
I downloaded the FreeBSD distribution ISO FreeBSD-9.0-powerpc-all.iso using your torrent.

I used Apple OS X 10.5.  I burned the rewriteable disk twice; once with Apple's Disk Utility and once with Toast 8.  The results are the same.

The problem is, when the disk is mounted, the kernel panics and the OS halts.  This happened for:
1. OS X 10.5 Server running on a PPC iBook
2. OS X 10.5 Workstation running on a PPC64 G5 Quad
3. OS X 10.7 Workstation running on an Intel 64bit MacBook

I do have FreeBSD systems, but I can't afford to bring them down just yet.  So this has NOT been replicated on FreeBSD.

This is very low priority for me, but I thought the issue should be raised for your attention.

How-To-Repeat: 1. Download FreeBSD-9.0-powerpc-all.iso
2. Burn it to CD using OS X's Disk Utility or Toast 8.
3. Mount it on Apple OS X.  The system halts.
